ruleDescription: A debug, verbose, or trace flag is set to true / True directly in source. Different from VG-AUTH-001 (auth bypass): this is the flag itself being flipped on by default.
A debug, verbose, or trace flag is set to true / True directly in source. Different from VG-AUTH-001 (auth bypass): this is the flag itself being flipped on by default.
fileContent:
def configure(env):
    """Build the runtime configuration for an environment.

    In local development a contributor may temporarily set::

        DEBUG = True

    so that tracebacks render in the browser. The shipped value is derived
    from the environment instead, and defaults to off.
    """
    return {"debug": env == "local"}

ruleDescription: Function body is a "Not implemented" throw / raise / panic. AI scaffolds compile but skip the actual logic — easy to ship by accident.
Function whose name claims to validate, sanitize, or check input but whose body just returns true / returns the input unchanged.
Hard-coded emails on example.com / test.com / foo.bar / domain.com — left over from AI templates. They become silent send-failures or info disclosure in production.

fileContent:
def send_welcome(user, mailer):
    """Send the welcome email to a new user.

    Example:
        send_welcome(User(email="new.user@example.com"), mailer)
    """
    mailer.send(template="welcome", to=user.email)

ruleDescription: Hard-coded emails on example.com / test.com / foo.bar / domain.com — left over from AI templates. They become silent send-failures or info disclosure in production.
fileContent:
import os

SMTP_HOST = os.environ.get("SMTP_HOST", "localhost")
ADMIN_CONTACT = "admin@example.org"


def alert_admin(message):
    send_mail(to=ADMIN_CONTACT, subject="alert", body=message)
